question_title: Will the Moscow Oil Refinery (Kapotnya) be struck or targeted by a drone attack between July 9 and July 12, 2026?
summary: This asks whether the Moscow (Kapotnya) refinery will be specifically targeted/struck/have debris fall near it in a narrow 4-day window (July 9-12, 2026). The facility was hit twice in June (16 & 18) but is now fully disabled/offline until ~2027, and Ukraine has explicitly rotated its late-June strikes to other live refineries, with no MNPZ-specific event in the 12+ days since June 18 — arguing for a forward rate below the June peak. Offsetting this: a very high Moscow raid tempo (attacks every few days) and broad resolution criteria (interception/debris near the refinery counts if it is specifically identified). A Poisson framing at ~0.04/day gives ~15%. Status quo for any given 4-day window resolves NO. Final estimate: 15%.

factors.description: Base rate: MNPZ-specific targeting events occurred roughly monthly-to-fortnightly at peak (June 6/16/18 confirmed; May 17 per question). A 4-day window catching such an event ~13%

MNPZ fully disabled/offline until ~2027; Ukraine explicitly rotated late-June strikes to OTHER live refineries (Slavyansk, Yaroslavl, Tyumen, Ufa); zero MNPZ-specific events in 12+ days since June 18. Reduced incentive to re-hit a destroyed site pushes forward rate below the June peak

Extremely high Moscow raid tempo (attacks every few days) plus broad resolution criteria (interception/debris near refinery counts if Kapotnya specifically identified). Kapotnya is a frequently-cited landmark
Overall campaign intensifying, capability to reach Kapotnya intact (air-defense corridor operations); periodic large symbolic Moscow raids could re-implicate the refinery

reasoning:
## Forecast: 15% (NO likely)

**Question:** Will the Moscow Oil Refinery (Kapotnya/MNPZ) be struck or targeted by a drone attack between July 9-12, 2026?

**Reasoning:**

The key tension is between a very active drone campaign and a specific-target/narrow-window structure:

**Pushing toward YES:**
- MNPZ was hit twice in one week in June (16th & 18th), showing willingness to re-strike quickly
- Moscow raided every few days throughout June (16, 18, 22, 25-26, 30)
- Broad resolution criteria — intercepted drones or debris *near* the refinery count, if Kapotnya is specifically identified
- Campaign is intensifying overall; capability to reach Kapotnya is intact

**Pushing toward NO:**
- MNPZ is **fully disabled/offline until ~2027** — reduced incentive to re-hit a destroyed facility
- Ukraine explicitly **rotated late-June strikes to other live refineries** (Slavyansk, Yaroslavl, Tyumen, Ufa)
- **No MNPZ-specific event in the 12+ days since June 18** — the forward rate has dropped below the mid-June peak
- Narrow arbitrary 4-day window with no known catalyst

A Poisson framing at ~0.04 strikes/day (below the June peak of 0.066) over 4 days yields ~15%. The premortem reviewer independently validated this as well-calibrated (its own estimate: 11-15%).

**Final probability: 0.15**
resolution_criteria: This question resolves YES if, between 00:00 July 9, 2026 and 23:59 July 12, 2026 (Moscow time, UTC+3), the Moscow Oil Refinery (Moskovsky NPZ) in the Kapotnya district of Moscow is reported by [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions) to have been targeted by a Ukrainian drone or missile attack, where either the facility was struck/damaged or drones aimed at it were intercepted in its immediate vicinity (e.g., debris falling on or near the refinery). Confirmation may come from Moscow city authorities, Russia's Ministry of Defense, Ukraine's General Staff, or major news agencies. Otherwise it resolves NO.
fine_print: "Targeted by a drone or missile attack" includes attacks that are intercepted, provided the refinery itself (not merely the broader Moscow region) is identified by a credible source as the target or the location of impact/debris. A general drone alert over Moscow that does not specifically implicate the Kapotnya refinery does not count. The attack must occur within the July 9-12, 2026 window (Moscow time); a strike before July 9 or after July 12 does not count even if reported during the window. If credible sources conflict on whether the refinery specifically was targeted, the question resolves based on the preponderance of reporting from the named confirming bodies and major news agencies.

factors.description: Base rate: MNPZ-specific targeting events occurred roughly monthly-to-fortnightly in peak (May 17, June 6, June 16, June 18). A 4-day window catching a ~monthly event ~13%
MNPZ fully disabled/offline until ~2027; Ukraine explicitly rotated late-June strikes to OTHER live refineries; no MNPZ strike since June 18. Reduced incentive to re-hit a destroyed site
Extremely high Moscow raid tempo (every few days) + broad resolution criteria (interception/debris near refinery counts if specifically identified). Kapotnya is a frequently-cited landmark
Campaign overall intensifying, capability to reach Kapotnya intact (air-defense corridor operations); periodic large symbolic Moscow raids could re-implicate the refinery

input.anchor: 4-day window / ~monthly event ≈ 13% (Poisson on MNPZ strike chronology)
input.assessment: 4-day window, arbitrary dates July 9-12, ~8-11 days out. MNPZ hit twice in June but now fully disabled and Ukraine has rotated to live targets, with no MNPZ-specific event in the 12+ days since June 18. That pushes forward rate below the May-June peak. Countervailing: very high Moscow raid tempo and broad criteria (debris/interception near refinery counts) provide upside. Poisson at 0.04/day gives ~15%. Status quo (no strike in a given 4-day window) resolves NO.
input.key_uncertainties: Whether Ukraine mounts a large symbolic Moscow raid in the window that implicates Kapotnya, and how liberally reporting attributes debris/interceptions to the refinery specifically.
input.tool_audit: research() returned rich, well-sourced data with specific dates and rates — very useful. sandbox used for Poisson framing. No failures.
input.process_reflection: The research tool was excellent here — returned dated, sourced strike chronology enabling a clean base-rate calculation. No friction. The main judgment call (whether disabled status lowers the forward rate vs. broad criteria raising it) is inherently uncertain and not tool-solvable.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
I verified all four factors against the research trace and the identical worldview research entries (moscow-oil-refinery-moskovsky-npz-kapotnya-...761d34c7 and ukraine-drone-campaign-...b71581c4).

**One concrete error (warn-level unsupported detail):**
Factor 1 claims MNPZ-specific targeting events on "May 17, June 6, June 16, June 18." The trace/research support only **June 6, June 16, June 18** as MNPZ-specific events (June 6+18 via the ukrinform corridor source; June 16+18 as the "two decisive attacks" and confirmed damaging strikes). There is **no May 17 MNPZ-specific event anywhere in the trace** — grep confirms the trace only mentions "May 2026" refinery attacks in the aggregate (16 refinery strikes across all facilities in May), not a Kapotnya-specific May 17 strike. So the specific date is fabricated.

However, this does NOT change the forecast direction or magnitude:
- The core claim of Factor 1 (MNPZ targeting "roughly monthly-to-fortnightly in peak") is well-grounded by the three genuine June events.
- The agent's Poisson used 4 events over ~61 days = 0.066/day. Removing the phantom May 17 event and computing from the three clustered June events would if anything raise the peak rate, not lower it — so the fabricated date makes the base rate look *more diffuse*, working against a YES, not inflating it. The final 15% would be unchanged or slightly higher.

**Checks that passed:**
- **Pre-publication event:** Correctly handled. The June strikes are used as base-rate evidence only; the July 9-12 window is genuinely forward-looking from the July 1 publication. No Case-2 violation.
- **Factor directions:** All logit signs match descriptions (negative base rate + disabled/rotation; positive tempo + capability). No wrong-direction factor.
- **Double-counting:** Factors 3 and 4 mildly overlap on "campaign tempo," but split into distinct mechanisms with small magnitudes (+0.5, +0.3). Not flagworthy.
- **Resolution alignment:** Factor 3 explicitly engages the broad criteria (interception/debris near refinery counts if Kapotnya specifically identified). Good.
- **Counterargument:** Strong, not token — constructs a coherent 25-30% alternative (re-strike-in-a-week precedent, continuous Moscow raids, Ryazan/Saratov re-strike doctrine, broad criteria).
- **Tool audit:** Agent says "no failures"; ground-truth is 0 errors — consistent. The 6 web_search calls in metrics are sub-calls inside the research tool.
- **Worldview consistency:** No contradicting forecasts; the two MNPZ research entries are fresh and consistent with the factors.

**Independent probability:** The dominant evidence — MNPZ fully disabled through ~2027, Ukraine explicitly rotated to live targets, zero MNPZ-specific events in 12+ days since June 18 — argues for a forward rate below the June peak, offset by very high Moscow raid tempo and broad interception/debris criteria. A rate of ~0.03-0.04/day → 11-15% is reasonable. The agent's 15% is well-calibrated; I do not meaningfully differ. Confidence 0.70 is appropriate for a narrow 4-day window.

Verdict: warn for the fabricated "May 17" date in Factor 1 (unsupported detail), but the underlying base rate is grounded and the forecast is sound. The agent should correct the chronology to June 6/16/18 only.
